evidences.title: Steam Community :: Slay the Spire 2
evidences.content: 1,530 70 Beta Patch Notes - v0.106.0 21 May It's time for another beta patch! A main focus of this one is enemy balance, including a rework of Infested Prism, more adjustments to Skulking Colony, and some changes to the new boss, Aeonglass. We've also got quite a few more Epoch art additions and more cool combat animations for some of the characters! Let's dive in... CONTENT & BALANCE General: If an enemy has Vulnerable and a player takes an extra turn via Pael's Eye relic, the enemy's Vulnerable amount no longer decrements before the player's extra turn + This change also applies to other powers that decrement at the end of the enemy's turn (Frail, Intangible, Weak, etc.) Ancients: Changed Neow's Scroll Boxes Silken Tress relics: swapped downsides and pools [...] Enemy Changes As many of you have heard by now, The Doormaker has been removed from the game. As we said in the patch notes of the beta patch that brought this change: while Doormaker had interesting micro decisions in the fight, he was over the complexity threshold of what we want and had lingering issues. We decided that starting over fresh will let us hit what we actually want for an Act 3 boss. Thus a new boss, Aeo...

evidences.title: Slay the Spire 2 Patch Notes v0.101.0 - StratGG
evidences.content: ### March 28, 2026 — Beta Patch v0.101.0 Reverted major nerfs to Prepared, Capture Spirit, and Borrowed Time. Major Regent buffs and Doormaker rework. ### March 19, 2026 — Beta Patch v0.100.0 First major post-launch balance patch on the beta branch. Main themes: fewer easy infinites, broader card reworks, accessibility improvements through Phobia Mode, relic skipping at chests, and merchant pricing changes. ### March 17, 2026 — Patch v0.99.1 (Main Branch) This patch pushed the previous beta updates live to the main branch. It added the Murderous run modifier icon, new portrait art for Feeding Frenzy, Sloth, and Waste Away, a max-HP cap for players, enemies, and pets, plus a large batch of crash, controller, cloud save, and multiplayer desync fixes. [...] Defect: Hotfix was nerfed hard, but Rocket Punch got a meaningful buff because its cost reduction now lasts until the card is actually played. ### Other Notable Changes Added new Neow blessings: Hefty Tablet and Neow's Talisman Gold-generating relics no longer appear at Merchants Several colorless cards and relics were buffed, including Seeker Strike, Eternal Armor, Book of Five Rings, and Stone Cracker Multiple enemy encounters...

evidences.title: Will both Aeonglass and Infested Prism receive balance changes in the next Slay the Spire 2 Beta Patch? | Manifold
evidences.content: This market will resolve to YES if both Aeonglass and Infested Prism receive direct changes under the "CONTENT & BALANCE" portion of the next Slay the Spire 2 Beta Branch Patch. This includes adjustments to things directly related to the boss itself, such as boss-exclusive powers and statuses, but not indirect changes, such as a buff or nerf to a card that performs well into either fight. Stuff like bug fixes or art updates will not count. This question is managed and resolved by Manifold. Market context Gaming Slay the Spire Get Ṁ1,000 to start trading! # 🏅 Top traders | # | Trader | Total profit | --- | 1 | Ṁ136 | | 2 | Ṁ13 | ## People are also trading When will Slay the Spire 2 be fully released? ## Related questions When will Slay the Spire 2 be fully released? [...] MANIFOLD Will both Aeonglass and Infested Prism receive balance changes in the next Slay the Spire 2 Beta Patch? 6 Ṁ1kṀ136 resolved Jun 5 Resolved NO 1H 6H 1D 1W 1M ALL This market will resolve based on the next non-hotfix Slay the Spire 2 Beta Branch Patch, with the next Beta Patch expected on June 4th. Any hotfixes or main branch patches will not count.

evidences.content: Mummified Hand ### Enemy and Boss Changes ## What was changed in the v0.106.1 hotfix? Runic Pyramid You can readthe full list of changes on Steam. Absey32 profile picture ### Absey32's impressions of the 0.105.0 Patch "Aeonglass previously allowed decks with broken draw engines to sidestep the card play restriction, but now it pressures you to end the fight, which I think is going to be very healthy for the game. Fasten and Shatter got the nerfs they deserved, and are probably still amazing. Synthesis received a good buff but I still wish there were better reasons to draft it. Infected Prism now actually demands some consideration when drafting, which is cool." Absey32 is a writer and streamer passionate about roguelites, arena shooters, consumer tech, and discovering music. [...] Untapped.gg Logo Slay the Spire 2 Logo # Slay the Spire 2 v0.106.0 Patch Notes - Beta Branch Update (Ironclad card buffs, Elite reworks) Luci Kelemen Slay the Spire 2’s May 22 beta patch, denoted v0.106.0, and next day's v0.106.1 hotfix, brought along another round of card buffs and nerfs for the game's experimental track alongside changes to various Elites and the new Aeonglass boss. evidences.title: Slay the Spire 2 roadmap locks in new character and acts, but matchmaking remains off the table | GosuGamers
evidences.content: ## Slay the Spire 2 beta branch and ongoing updates Recent patches spanning v0.100.0 to v0.103.1 have already delivered balance changes, UI updates, writing improvements, and bug fixes. A beta branch remains available for players who want early access to experimental changes before they are pushed to the main build, with updates rolling out more frequently than standard patches. As Early Access continues, Mega Crit’s focus remains on refining systems first, then layering in larger content expansions over time. And while some of these changes seem contentious at first, not everything is set in stone just yet. Author [...] Release date: 5 March 2026 (Early Access) Platforms: PC(Steam), MacOS, and Linux Developer: Megacrit ## What is included in the Slay the Spire 2 roadmap? Mega Crit confirmed the first month of Early Access has largely focused on stabilising the game. “We’ve been fixing up major issues, improving our feedback systems, polishing up visuals, and reworking/balancing content so that all of the cards and items feel interesting and viable,” the team said. On the systems side, one of the biggest additions planned is Steam Workshop support, opening the door for modding t...

evidences.title: Slay the Spire 2 Patch Notes v0.101.0 - StratGG
evidences.content: StratGG StratGG # Slay the Spire 2 Patch Notes Last updated: March 29, 2026 Dates on this page follow the Steam announcement timeline used for the U.S. storefront. Quick Answer Slay the Spire 2 launched in Early Access on March 5, 2026. For ongoing news flow, follow Slay the Spire 2 news. Mega Crit is actively shipping hotfixes and balance patches. The newest major update is Beta Patch v0.101.0 from March 28, 2026, which controversially reverts several major v0.100.0 nerfs and reworks Regent. This page tracks every official update — bookmark it to stay current. ## Latest Patch [...] ### March 28, 2026 — The Great Reversion Mega Crit quickly responded to widespread community feedback regarding the drastic nerfs in v0.100.0. This beta patch fully reverts the controversial changes to Prepared (Silent) and Capture Spirit / Borrowed Time (Necrobinder). It also heavily buffs the Regent, reworks the Doormaker boss, and improves the feedback system. ### Key Reverts & Buffs Prepare (cost 1, discard 2, refund energy) has reverted back to its original 0-cost draw/discard form. Necrobinder's Borrowed Time and Capture Spirit have been fully restored to their pre-nerf strength. Regent's Arsen...

evidences.title: Slay The Spire 2's Latest Patch A Wild Mix Of Card Nerfs ...
evidences.content: The latest patch is, as usual, pretty chunky, as it includes reworks for some of Neow’s Ancient relics, a mix of nerfs and buffs for cards, some fresh animations, and a couple of reworked Elite fights. The Elite changes are the real headliners here, though. The Skulking Colony Elite received a relatively sizeable nerf, as it can now take a minimum of 20 damage per turn (up from 15) and it no longer gains block on any of its turns. But Mega Crit giveth and Mega Crit taketh away, because the buffs to the Infested Prism Elite are downright nasty. [...] Infested Prism now has a new gimmick; whenever you play a skill card against this Elite, you will temporarily become “Tainted.” It starts with you accumulating 3 Tainted per skill, but Infested Prism will increase this in increments of 3 every few turns. Tainted effectively grants Infested Prism a temporary strength buff, which means that if it hits you with a multi-hit attack and you’ve accumulated, for instance, 6 Tainted, every single individual hit will do 6 more damage. Basically, this Elite is a damage race now, and you’re probably going to want to make sure you kill it within four turns as a result. [...] # Slay The Spire 2′s...
